closed_children.title: Forward-compat hardening: symmetric unknown=round-trip across all three serde seams

description:
The architecture is already extensible: one Negotiation primitive parameterized over a wire protocol; a Participant trait carrying EventCtx plus propose/fetch_remote_view/detect_conflict (SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants.md S5/S7). The SUBPROCESS WIRE is not. runner.rs sends only the post-image Task JSON + --event. The internal EventCtx (S5) never reaches the plugin; there is no first-class reject; task birth and drop have no event at all.

GOAL: turn "observe a transition and contribute fields" into "run arbitrary policy and arbitrary side-effects on ANY transition" with no new state store, no protocol fork, and zero change to legacy plugins. This extends the bl-8b71 native-protocol surface and AMENDS SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants.md (S5 EventCtx wire delivery, S6 event set, S8 reject-vs-conflict-vs-Other) rather than introducing a new SPEC; the amendment lands in the prereq child.

THE ONE RULE: "unknown = round-trip, never die." Today this is stated only for Task fields (S13 / bl-d31c extra map). Apply it SYMMETRICALLY across three serde seams: Task fields (done), native-response variants, and the describe subscription set. Every addition rides describe-gated negotiation; every new bl->plugin input rides a describe-gated SIDE CHANNEL (the BALLS_DIAG_FD dedicated-FD precedent), NEVER the legacy stdin shape — wrapping stdin breaks conformance S17.1.

HONEST BOUNDARY (the design working, not a gap): legacy non-native plugins can never receive EventCtx/Reject/create — they have no describe handshake to gate on, and their stdin is frozen by S17.1. Extensibility is native-only BY CONSTRUCTION; legacy stays frozen, consistent with S8's existing stance that legacy plugins do not get the retry-on-conflict path. The compat boundary is intentional, not leaking.

WHY: every real integration is impossible on today's wire because the plugin gets the post-image with no diff, no actor, no veto, and no birth/death event — Slack "who moved bl-1234 to review", CI-gated close, Jira mirror-on-create, "agent walked away" on drop. Closing this makes bl a real integration substrate while preserving the clean default and the S2 soft-policy invariant: a required plugin can veto via Reject, but an explicit operator override still ships (soft policy, hard primitive).
title: Native-protocol extensibility: arbitrary policy and side-effects on any transition
